artifacts.claimText: University of Colombo states that its generative AI policy applies to the use of GenAI tools in academic and administrative activities.
artifacts.normalizedValue: GenAI policy applies to academic and administrative activities.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities

artifacts.claimText: University of Colombo requires students and staff to remain responsible for work involving AI and to review, fact-check, and edit AI-generated content before using it in academic or administrative work.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Users remain responsible and must verify AI-generated content before incorporation.

artifacts.claimText: For student work, University of Colombo says AI uses beyond citation/reference generation and accessibility support must be explicitly acknowledged, and that the original intellectual work, analysis, interpretation, and synthesis must be the student's own.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Student AI use beyond specified no-declaration uses requires acknowledgement and student-owned intellectual work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This policy applies to the use of generative AI (GenAI) tools in academic and administrative activities. GenAI is broadly defined as "systems that possess the ability to process data in a way that resembles human intelligence" such as resembling human reasoning, learning, or creativity.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy states that it applies to GenAI tools in academic and administrative activities.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students and staff remain fully responsible for the accuracy, originality, quality and integrity of all completed works, regardless of whether AI tools were used or not. AI can assist their works, but it cannot replace their own critical analysis and intellectual contribution. Students and staff are required to review, fact-check, and edit any AI-generated content before incorporating it into academic or administrative works.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students and staff remain responsible for AI-assisted work and must review, fact-check, and edit AI-generated content before use.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Overarching principles for the use of GenAI, Accountability and Essential verification
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If students use AI for the purposes beyond those specified in 1 - 2 above, they must explicitly acknowledge such use of AI in their work. The declaration should include a short description as to how the tool was used, name and version of the AI system used, the scope or purpose of use, preferably with the AI prompt or type of input provided. In all instances, the original intellectual work, analysis, interpretation, and synthesis must be carried out by the student.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Student AI use beyond specified no-declaration uses must be acknowledged, and original intellectual work must remain the student's own.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Part I - Use of AI for Students, Permitted use of AI with declaration/acknowledgement
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Using AI tools in all assessments (summative, formative, self-assessment, continuous assessments, in-class assessments, final assessments, etc.) is not permitted, unless instructions explicitly authorise such use in a particular academic activity/question/assignment. In the absence of such permission to use AI tools, it should be considered that the use of AI is not permitted. Using AI tools in summative assessment without authorisation is considered cheating and tantamount to an examination offence similar to the use of unauthorized material at an examination.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI use in assessments is prohibited unless explicitly authorized, and unauthorized summative-assessment AI use is treated as cheating.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Part I - Use of AI for Students, Non-permitted/prohibited use of AI and punitive consequences
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI tools must not be used to mark or grade student works, generate evaluative feedback, or contribute to summative assessment decisions. AI systems/tools may only be used in assessment processes where the tool has undergone institutional review, rigorous validation and quality assurance, and where meaningful human oversight is maintained. Assessment decisions must remain fully human-led unless these checks are in place.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI marking, feedback, or summative assessment support is not allowed unless institutional review, validation, quality assurance, and human oversight are in place.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Part II - Use of AI in Teaching and Research by Staff, Non-permitted use of AI in teaching
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence in Academic and Administrative Activities
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Sharing with/uploading of sensitive or confidential information to an AI tool or any online sharing platform is strictly prohibited. Students must never input, upload, or share sensitive, confidential, or personally identifiable information (PII) into any AI tool or online platform. Staff must never input, upload, or share sensitive, confidential, or personally identifiable information (PII) into any AI tool or online platform.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students and staff must not upload or share sensitive, confidential, or personally identifiable information into AI tools or online platforms.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Part I and Part III data protection, privacy, and confidentiality sections
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow scope claim directly supported by the official policy PDF.
Approved because the policy expressly states both accountability and essential verification duties.
Approved as a student-use claim limited to the acknowledgement section and student intellectual-work requirement.
Approved because the policy explicitly prohibits AI in assessments absent authorization and characterizes unauthorized summative-assessment use as cheating.
Approved with the institutional review, validation, quality assurance, and human oversight conditions preserved.
Approved because the policy separately prohibits students and staff from uploading or sharing sensitive, confidential, or PII data into AI tools or online platforms.

artifacts.title: University of Colombo Generative AI Policy Candidate
artifacts.summary: Staged agent-reviewed claim/evidence candidates for the University of Colombo's official policy on generative AI in academic and administrative activities. Claims are based on the official policy PDF and scoped to policy scope, accountability and verification, student acknowledgement, unauthorized assessment use, AI in teaching assessment, and privacy/confidentiality restrictions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The central CQA page links a Google Drive copy of the policy; claim evidence in this bundle uses the publicly fetchable official science.cmb.ac.lk PDF copy.
Repository scholarship and search-result pages were not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
